title: aberemoa jonasiana
side_effects: Aberemoa jonasiana can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or roots can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application can cause redness, itching, and rashes. Long-term exposure to the plant's toxins can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as increased risk of infections and diseases. Improper use of Aberemoa jonasiana can also interact with other medications, exacerbating existing health conditions.
